title: Delivery OS — Infrastructure Config Registry
description: The single declarative source of truth for one service's environment configuration. Every config key the build/runtime validates is DECLARED here (name, purpose, owning platform, requiredness per environment, a validation rule, and a redacted example). The config-doctor reads this to report PRESENT / MISSING / INVALID per key with an exact fix, and the deploy workflow gates on it. Derive the keys from the real code (e.g. the zod env schema) so the registry matches exactly what the build validates.

environments.description: The environments this service declares config for.

platforms.description: Where the config-doctor reads live state from. All ids are read from env (never committed secrets).

orgIdEnv.description: Env var holding the Vercel team/org id (default VERCEL_ORG_ID).

projectIdEnv.description: Env var holding the Vercel project id (default VERCEL_PROJECT_ID).

tokenEnv.description: Env var holding the read-only Vercel token (default VERCEL_TOKEN).

repo.description: owner/repo slug for `gh secret list` presence checks.

name.description: The env var name, exactly as the code reads it.

purpose.description: What it is for, in one line.

owner.description: The platform that OWNS/sources the value. 'vercel-env' and 'supabase' both live as Vercel env vars at runtime (supabase = sourced from the Supabase dashboard, installed into Vercel); 'github-secret' lives in GitHub Actions secrets.

rule.description: Validation rule id understood by config-doctor: postgres-pooler-6543 | postgres-url | url | non-empty | flag | int-positive | secret-min:N | enum:a|b|c. Use 'int-positive' for numeric tuning knobs (pool size, a timeout in ms/s) — it is the ENV-ROBUSTNESS STANDARD: an empty/0/non-numeric value FAILS the gate up front instead of silently coercing to 0 at runtime (the BUG-209-1 trap, where Number("")===0 disabled a serverless DB client's statement_timeout/pool bound). Its code-side companion is Number(env)||default (never Number(env ?? default)), asserted by platform-health's preflight-db-client.

required.description: true/false (all environments) OR an object keyed by environment name with boolean values.

example.description: A REDACTED example value — never a real secret.

fix.description: The exact, actionable remediation printed when this key is MISSING/INVALID.
